item.title: U.S. eases restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos AI model
item.description: The Trump administration is allowing Anthropic to restore access to its Mythos 5 AI model for a select group of U.S. companies and federal agencies. New York Times tech reporter Sheera Frenkel joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Utah mayor on wildfires: "Wrapping our heads around the damage"
item.description: Matt Robinson, the mayor of Beaver, Utah, joins CBS News to discuss the ongoing wildfires that have been burning across the state.

item.title: Path forward for U.S.-Iran talks unclear after weekend strikes
item.description: Iran denied its negotiators would be meeting with U.S. officials in Qatar after President Trump announced the talks would resume at Tehran's request.

item.title: Watch: Trump takes questions on Supreme Court rulings over mail-in ballots, presidential firings
item.description: President Trump spoke to reporters in the Oval Office Monday hours after the Supreme Court handed down landmark decisions on mail-in voting and presidential powers. Mr. Trump also discussed the SAVE Act.

item.title: 6 killed, suspect arrested in shooting at German youth center, police say
item.description: German police say a suspect was in custody and six were killed in a rare shooting that took place at a youth center in Stade, near Hamburg.

item.title: A timeline of the Karen Read case
item.description: A look at the timeline of events in Karen Read's high-profile Massachusetts murder trial and retrial. Read was acquitted of killing her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O'Keefe.

item.title: 3 critical mistakes every saver should avoid this July
item.description: In today's still-elevated interest rate climate, savers need to avoid making these three critical mistakes.

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump bid to fire Fed's Lisa Cook as legal case continues
item.description: The Supreme Court allowed Lisa Cook to continue in her post as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors while legal proceedings over President Trump's attempt to fire her continue.

item.title: Here's what to know about Medicare's new GLP-1 weight-loss drug program
item.description: For the first time, Medicare will cover GLP-1 drugs prescribed solely for weight loss for eligible beneficiaries at a $50 monthly copay.

item.title: Archdiocese of San Francisco files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy over child abuse lawsuits
item.description: The Archdiocese of San Francisco is officially filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in the wake of numerous claims of child sexual abuse allegedly committed by priests filed against it, according to an announcement Monday.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Supreme Court expands presidential firing power, overturning 90-year-old ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court overturned a 90-year-old decision that allowed Congress to shield members of certain independent agencies from being fired by the president at will.

item.title: Supreme Court says states can count mail ballots that arrive after Election Day
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday ruled that states can count mail ballots that are cast by Election Day but arrive later, rejecting a GOP challenge to a Mississippi law.

item.title: Poll: America at 250 and some of what Americans think is best about U.S.
item.description: Americans weighed in on what's best about the U.S., its greatest invention, most representative food and more in latest CBS News poll.

item.title: How much interest can an $80,000 CD account earn now?
item.description: Interest earnings made with an $80,000 CD account will be substantial and available relatively quickly.

item.title: Iran war has cost Americans $1,000 per household, economist estimates
item.description: The Iran war has significantly driven up the cost of fuel, airfare and other U.S. goods, according to Moody's Analytics chief economist Mark Zandi.

item.title: Aide who planned JFK Jr.'s secret wedding weighs in on Swift-Kelce rumors
item.description: RoseMarie Terenzio, who was JFK Jr.'s former chief of staff and planned his secret wedding to Carolyn Bessette, said she doesn't think Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are getting married at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: Breaking down Supreme Court's decisions on presidential firings, mail-in ballots
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday expanded the president's ability to fire heads of independent agencies, but not the Federal Reserve. It also ruled states can count mail ballots that are cast by Election Day but arrive later. CBS News' Jessica Levinson and Weijia Jiang have more.

item.title: Summer music highlights of 2026
item.description: Summer is the time to enjoy live music, indoors and out. Scroll through our gallery of some of 2026's leading musical acts, featuring images by CBS News photojournalist Jake Barlow and photographers Ed Spinelli and Kirstine Walton.

item.title: The Trump administration says SNAP is rife with fraud and waste. Is it?
item.description: The USDA says almost 11% of SNAP payments contain errors, almost double the threshold set by Congress. Here's what is going on.

item.title: Should you negotiate with debt collectors? 6 tips to improve your chances
item.description: Negotiations are typically less stressful if you know how to improve the odds of reaching a settlement agreement.

item.title: Senate Ethics Committee dismisses misconduct complaint against Gallego
item.description: The bipartisan committee said it "did not find evidence that your actions violated federal law, Senate rules or related standards of conduct."

item.title: Home equity loan vs. HELOC: Which option is right for your credit card payoff plan?
item.description: Both home equity borrowing options can be smart card debt payoff tools, but it's important to know which one is best.

item.title: Federal trial for Luigi Mangione pushed back to January 2027
item.description: A judge has pushed Luigi Mangione's federal trial date back to January of 2027. Mangione is accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. CBS News' Anna Schecter has more on the Mangione case and the indictment against NBA veteran Malik Beasley on gambling charges.

item.title: Rescue teams continue to find survivors of Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Tens of thousands of people are still presumed to be missing after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela last week.

item.title: Former NFL star Chris Johnson says he's been diagnosed with ALS
item.description: Former NFL running back Chris Johnson announced that he was diagnosed with ALS, also known as Lou Gehrig's disease, in a "Good Morning America" interview.

item.title: 12-year-old injured by bison at Yellowstone National Park
item.description: A 12-year-old was injured by a bison while visiting Yellowstone National Park last week, the National Park Service said.

item.title: Soccer star's wife and 2 children killed in Venezuela earthquakes, team says
item.description: The wife and two children of Argentine soccer star Lucas Trejo died after powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela, his team said.

item.title: Pablo Escobar murder victim's son is named Colombia's interior minister
item.description: The incoming minister's father, Rodrigo Lara Bonilla, was serving as justice minister in 1984 when he was gunned down in Bogota on Pablo Escobar's orders.

item.title: CBS to host "The Great American Block Party 250" special on July 4
item.description: CBS will host a primetime special celebrating America's 250th birthday on Saturday, July 4, with exclusive performances and the largest fireworks show in history.
